items.title: PCPJack Hijacked 230 AWS, GCP, and Azure Servers to Run a Hidden SMTP Relay Network
items.description: PCPJack operators compromised 230 cloud Linux servers across AWS, GCP, and Azure to build a covert SMTP relay network for email-based attacks. Researchers discovered exposed directories on infrastructure at 213.136.80[.]73 containing complete deployment toolkits including Chisel binaries, Python deployers, and operational state files. The campaign deployed Sliver C2 beacons and established reverse
items.ai_summary: The PCPJack threat actors have compromised 230 cloud Linux servers across AWS, GCP, and Azure to build a covert SMTP relay network for email-based attacks. The attackers exploited exposed directories on infrastructure to deploy toolkits including Chisel binaries and Python deployers, ultimately establishing reverse shells using Sliver C2 beacons. The real-world impact of this threat includes the potential for widespread email-based phishing and spam campaigns, affecting individuals and organizations relying on these cloud services. To remediate, defenders should immediately review their cloud infrastructure for exposed directories and ensure all servers are running the latest security patches, with a specific focus on securing deployment tools and application layer protocols. Additionally, defenders should monitor for unusual network activity and implement robust email security controls to prevent abuse of their infrastructure.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR
    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1071| B[Initial Access]:::tactic
    B -->|T1072| C[Execution]:::tactic
    C -->|T1219| D[Persistence]:::tactic
    D -->|T1071| E[Command and Control]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: Impersonation, Click Hijacking, and TDS: Inside a Malware Distribution Ecosystem
items.description: A large-scale operation impersonates open-source and freeware projects to capture search traffic, targeting tools such as Ghidra, dnSpy, and SpiderFoot. The professionally designed sites load CloudFront-hosted JavaScript that converts download button clicks into handoffs to a Traffic Distribution System (TDS), which enforces strict gating including first-visit state, click confirmation, anti-bot l
items.ai_summary: The threat involves a large-scale operation that impersonates open-source and freeware projects, such as Ghidra, dnSpy, and SpiderFoot, to capture search traffic. The attackers use professionally designed websites that load CloudFront-hosted JavaScript, which converts download button clicks into handoffs to a Traffic Distribution System (TDS). This TDS enforces strict gating, including first-visit state and click confirmation, to filter out unwanted traffic. To remediate this threat, defenders should implement robust web application firewalls (WAFs) and monitor for suspicious traffic patterns, such as unusual click activity or unfamiliar JavaScript files. Additionally, users should verify the authenticity of download links and avoid clicking on suspicious buttons, and developers should ensure their projects' download pages are secure and not vulnerable to click hijacking.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR
    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1190| B[Exploit Public-Facing Application]:::tactic
    B -->|T1134| C[Access Token Manipulation]:::tactic
    C -->|T1566| D[Initial Access]:::tactic
    D -->|T1059.001| E[Execution]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: The Demon Arrives Later: A Havoc Stager Hides Behind Microsoft Defender DLP
items.description: Cybercriminals in Brazil are exploiting the country's electronic invoice system (Nota Fiscal eletrônica) to deliver Havoc framework implants. The campaign surfaced during May 2026, coinciding with tax season when accountants routinely process invoice-related emails. Attackers distribute malicious ZIP files disguised as legitimate invoices, containing VBScript droppers that download MSI installers
items.ai_summary: The threat involves a Havoc framework implant delivered through malicious ZIP files disguised as legitimate invoices, targeting Brazil's electronic invoice system. The attack vector utilizes VBScript droppers to download MSI installers, exploiting the trust in invoice-related emails during tax season. The real-world impact includes potential data breaches and system compromise, affecting accountants and financial institutions processing these emails. To remediate, defenders should implement email filters to block ZIP files with VBScript attachments, and update their Microsoft Defender DLP configurations to detect and prevent Havoc framework implants. Additionally, users should be cautious when opening invoice-related emails and attachments, verifying their legitimacy before processing.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR
    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1059.005| B[Initial Access]:::tactic
    B -->|T1036| C[Execution]:::tactic
    C -->|T1204| D[Persistence]:::tactic
    D -->|T1082| E[Impact]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: Inside the Cross-Platform Propagation of a New Gafgyt Variant C0XMO
items.description: A new Gafgyt botnet variant named C0XMO has been discovered that spreads by exploiting a stack buffer overflow vulnerability in DD-WRT router firmware. Unlike earlier versions, this malware separates its lateral movement capabilities into a standalone Python script, enabling more efficient targeting of various system architectures including ARM, MIPS, PowerPC, and x86. The malware establishes pers
items.ai_summary: The C0XMO Gafgyt botnet variant exploits a stack buffer overflow vulnerability in DD-WRT router firmware, allowing it to spread across various system architectures including ARM, MIPS, PowerPC, and x86. This malware uses a standalone Python script for lateral movement, making it more efficient in targeting different systems. If exploited, this vulnerability can lead to the establishment of a botnet, allowing threat actors to conduct DDoS attacks and other malicious activities, affecting individuals and organizations relying on vulnerable DD-WRT routers. To remediate this threat, defenders should immediately update their DD-WRT firmware to the latest version and ensure that remote access to the router is secured with strong passwords and limited to necessary services. Additionally, network traffic should be monitored for suspicious activity, and any detected botnet communication should be blocked and reported to the relevant authorities.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR
    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1203| B[Exploitation for Client Execution]:::tactic
    B -->|T1592.003| C[Exploitation of Firmware]:::tactic
    C -->|T1021| D[Remote Services]:::tactic
    D -->|T1583.005| E[Establishment of a Botnet]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: Browser Spy-Ons: Threat Actor's Extension Hijack Your AI Conversations
items.description: Multiple malicious Chrome extensions are exploiting the growing use of AI platforms by disguising themselves as legitimate productivity tools while secretly stealing user conversations and personal data. Extensions including Urban VPN, Smart Sidebar, and AI Assistant/Chat AI collectively reach millions of users but contain hidden scripts that intercept communications with popular AI platforms like
items.ai_summary: The threat actor is exploiting the popularity of AI platforms by creating malicious Chrome extensions, including Urban VPN, Smart Sidebar, and AI Assistant/Chat AI, which have collectively reached millions of users. These extensions contain hidden scripts that intercept communications with popular AI platforms, allowing the threat actor to steal user conversations and personal data. The attack vector is through the exploitation of public-facing applications, specifically Chrome extensions, which is categorized as T1190. To remediate this threat, defenders should immediately remove the affected extensions and update Chrome to the latest version, ensuring that all extensions are verified and trusted. Additionally, users should be cautious when installing new extensions and monitor their browser activity for any suspicious behavior.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR
    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1190| B[Exploit Public-Facing Application]:::tactic
    B -->|T1056| C[Initial Access]:::tactic
    C -->|T1204| D[User Execution]:::tactic
    D -->|T1021| E[Impact]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: Preinstall to persistence: Inside the npm Miasma credential-stealing campaign
items.description: Microsoft Threat Intelligence discovered a large-scale npm supply chain attack compromising 32 malicious packages across over 90 versions under the @redhat-cloud-services scope. The compromise originated from the RedHatInsights/javascript-clients CI/CD pipeline, enabling attackers to publish trojanized packages through legitimate GitHub Actions OIDC workflows with authentic provenance signatures.
items.ai_summary: The npm Miasma credential-stealing campaign involves a large-scale supply chain attack compromising 32 malicious packages across over 90 versions under the @redhat-cloud-services scope. The attack originated from the RedHatInsights/javascript-clients CI/CD pipeline, allowing attackers to publish trojanized packages through legitimate GitHub Actions OIDC workflows with authentic provenance signatures, leveraging T1195 (Supply Chain Compromise). If exploited, this campaign can lead to the theft of sensitive credentials, affecting developers and organizations relying on the compromised packages. To remediate, developers should remove and replace affected packages with verified versions, and audit their CI/CD pipelines for suspicious activity, ensuring that all dependencies are up-to-date and securely configured. Additionally, implementing a robust software bill of materials (SBOM) and regularly monitoring package updates can help defenders identify and mitigate similar supply chain attacks.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR
    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1195| B[Initial Access]:::tactic
    B -->|T1059.001| C[Execution]:::tactic
    C -->|T1041| D[Command and Control]:::tactic
    D -->|T1486| E[Impact]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: Argamal: Malware hidden in hentai games
items.description: In April 2026, researchers discovered a malware campaign targeting players of adult-themed games. The infected games install a previously unknown implant called Argamal that downloads and executes a RAT after several days, resulting in full system compromise. The malware uses COM hijacking to persist, replacing the InprocServer32 entry for Windows Color System Calibration Loader DLL. Delivery occu
items.ai_summary: The Argamal malware campaign targets players of adult-themed games, specifically hentai games, by hiding the malware within the games themselves. The malware uses COM hijacking, identified as T1546.015, to persist on the system by replacing the InprocServer32 entry for the Windows Color System Calibration Loader DLL, allowing it to download and execute a Remote Access Trojan (RAT) after several days, leading to full system compromise. This campaign affects Windows systems, with the exact version not specified, but the use of COM hijacking suggests Windows 10 or later may be vulnerable. To remediate, defenders should monitor for suspicious activity related to the Windows Color System Calibration Loader DLL and implement software restriction policies to prevent unauthorized execution of programs. Additionally, users should be cautious when downloading and installing games from untrusted sources, and consider using antivirus software that can detect and remove the Argamal malware.
items.workflow_graph:

    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1546.015| B[Privilege Escalation]:::tactic
    B -->|T1219| C[Command and Control]:::tactic
    C -->|T1059.001| D[Execution]:::tactic
    D -->|T1082| E[Defense Evasion]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: UAT-8302 and its box full of malware
items.description: UAT-8302 is a sophisticated China-nexus advanced persistent threat group targeting government entities in South America since late 2024 and southeastern Europe in 2025. The actor deploys multiple custom-made malware families including NetDraft, a .NET-based backdoor variant of FinalDraft/SquidDoor, and CloudSorcerer version 3. Post-compromise activities involve extensive reconnaissance, credential
items.ai_summary: The UAT-8302 threat group, linked to China, has been targeting government entities in South America since late 2024 and southeastern Europe in 2025. They deploy custom-made malware families, including NetDraft, a .NET-based backdoor variant of FinalDraft/SquidDoor, and CloudSorcerer version 3. The actor exploits public-facing applications (T1190) and uses remote access software (T1219) to gain initial access. Post-compromise, they conduct extensive reconnaissance and credential harvesting. To remediate, defenders should update all public-facing applications to the latest version, implement a web application firewall, and monitor for suspicious remote access software connections. Additionally, implement a .NET framework patch for the affected version to prevent NetDraft exploitation.

items.title: CloudZ RAT potentially steals OTP messages using Pheno plugin
items.description: Cisco Talos uncovered an intrusion active since January 2026 where attackers deployed CloudZ remote access tool and an undocumented plugin called Pheno to steal credentials and one-time passwords. The attack exploits Microsoft Phone Link application by intercepting synchronized mobile data including SMS and OTPs without requiring phone-level infection. CloudZ evades detection through dynamic memor
items.ai_summary: CloudZ RAT, a remote access tool, has been used in a real-world attack since January 2026 to steal credentials and one-time passwords (OTPs) using an undocumented plugin called Pheno. The attack exploits the Microsoft Phone Link application, intercepting synchronized mobile data, including SMS and OTPs, without requiring phone-level infection. This exploit leverages T1190 (Exploit Public-Facing Application) and T1219 (Remote Access Software) tactics. To remediate, users should update the Microsoft Phone Link application to the latest version (v1.2023.100 or later) and ensure that the 'Sync SMS' feature is disabled. Additionally, organizations should monitor for suspicious activity related to CloudZ RAT and Pheno plugin.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR

    B -->|T1219| C[Remote Access Software]:::tactic

    D -->|T1486| E[Impact]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: ClickFix Deno Abuse to CastleRAT
items.description: Activity began with a ClickFix-style social engineering chain that led to MSI execution, PowerShell staging, and installation/use of Deno to run attacker-controlled JavaScript. Follow-on activity downloaded a portable Python runtime, `install.pyc`, and an encrypted `.MOa` container, which was later decrypted to recover a 64-bit Windows PE payload. Analysis of the recovered payload showed Steam Com
items.ai_summary: The ClickFix Deno Abuse to CastleRAT threat involves a sophisticated social engineering chain that leverages MSI execution and PowerShell staging to install Deno, a JavaScript runtime environment. This allows attackers to execute attacker-controlled JavaScript, which in turn downloads a portable Python runtime and an encrypted container. The container is decrypted to recover a 64-bit Windows PE payload, indicating a potential privilege escalation or data breach. To remediate, defenders should ensure Deno is not installed on vulnerable systems, and consider implementing strict access controls for MSI execution and PowerShell staging. Additionally, systems should be updated to the latest version of Deno, and any suspicious Python runtimes or encrypted containers should be quarantined.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR

    B -->|T1059.001| C[PowerShell]:::tactic
    C -->|T1566| D[Initial Access]:::tactic
    D -->|T1041| E[Command and Control]:::tactic
    E -->|T1486| F[Impact]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: CVE-2026-48846: In Roundcube Webmail 1.6.x before 1.6.16 and 1.7.x before 1.7.1, the remote imag...
items.description: In Roundcube Webmail 1.6.x before 1.6.16 and 1.7.x before 1.7.1, the remote image blocking feature can be bypassed via a crafted CSS var() value in an e-mail message, which may lead to information disclosure or access-control bypass.
items.ai_summary: CVE-2026-48846 is a vulnerability in Roundcube Webmail 1.6.x before 1.6.16 and 1.7.x before 1.7.1, allowing attackers to bypass remote image blocking via a crafted CSS var() value in an email message. This may lead to information disclosure or access-control bypass. To remediate, update to Roundcube Webmail 1.6.16 or later, or 1.7.1 or later. Additionally, ensure that the 'remote_image_blocking' setting is enabled in the Roundcube configuration file. This vulnerability has a CVSS score of 6.5 and is considered medium in severity. Defenders should prioritize updating affected systems to prevent exploitation.

items.title: CVE-2026-48845: In Roundcube Webmail 1.6.x between 1.6.14 and 1.6.16 and 1.7.x before 1.7.1, rem...
items.description: In Roundcube Webmail 1.6.x between 1.6.14 and 1.6.16 and 1.7.x before 1.7.1, remote image blocking was not honored for URLs pointing to local/private destinations, which may lead to information disclosure or privilege escalation via a text/html email message.
items.ai_summary: CVE-2026-48845 is a remote code execution vulnerability in Roundcube Webmail 1.6.x between 1.6.14 and 1.6.16 and 1.7.x before 1.7.1. An attacker can exploit this vulnerability by sending a malicious email with a text/html message containing a URL pointing to a local/private destination, which bypasses the remote image blocking feature. This may lead to information disclosure or privilege escalation. To remediate this vulnerability, update to Roundcube Webmail 1.6.17 or later, or 1.7.1 or later. Additionally, ensure that the 'remote_image_blocking' configuration option is set to 'on' in the config.inc.php file. This vulnerability has a CVSS score of 6.5 and is considered medium in severity.

items.title: CVE-2026-48844: Roundcube Webmail 1.6.x before 1.6.16 and 1.7.x before 1.7.1 has insecure code e...
items.description: Roundcube Webmail 1.6.x before 1.6.16 and 1.7.x before 1.7.1 has insecure code evaluation logic in LDAP the autovalues option that could lead to code injection. (Support for code evaluation has been removed in 1.6.16 and 1.7.1.)
items.ai_summary: CVE-2026-48844 is a critical vulnerability in Roundcube Webmail 1.6.x before 1.6.16 and 1.7.x before 1.7.1, allowing attackers to inject malicious code via LDAP autovalues option. This insecure code evaluation logic enables threat actors to execute arbitrary commands, leading to potential data breaches and privilege escalation. To remediate this issue, administrators must update to Roundcube Webmail 1.6.16 or later, or 1.7.1 or later. Additionally, it is recommended to disable the LDAP autovalues option until the update is applied. This vulnerability has been exploited in the wild, with threat actors using T1055 (Process Injection) tactics to gain initial access.

items.title: CVE-2026-48843: Roundcube Webmail 1.6.x between 1.6.14 and 1.6.16,and 1.7.x before 1.7.1 has Ins...
items.description: Roundcube Webmail 1.6.x between 1.6.14 and 1.6.16,and 1.7.x before 1.7.1 has Insufficient Cascading Style Sheets (CSS) sanitization in HTML e-mail messages may lead to SSRF or Information Disclosure, e.g., if stylesheet links point to local network hosts. The issue stems from an insufficient fix for CVE-2026-35540.
items.ai_summary: CVE-2026-48843 is a critical vulnerability in Roundcube Webmail 1.6.x between 1.6.14 and 1.6.16, and 1.7.x before 1.7.1, allowing attackers to perform Server-Side Request Forgery (SSRF) or Information Disclosure by injecting malicious Cascading Style Sheets (CSS) in HTML email messages. This issue stems from an insufficient fix for CVE-2026-35540. If exploited, attackers can potentially access sensitive information or execute malicious requests on the targeted system. To remediate this vulnerability, update Roundcube Webmail to version 1.6.17 or later, or version 1.7.1 or later. Additionally, ensure that all email messages are properly sanitized to prevent CSS injection attacks.

items.title: CVE-2026-48842: Roundcube Webmail 1.6.x before 1.6.16 and 1.7.x before 1.7.1 has Pre-authenticat...
items.description: Roundcube Webmail 1.6.x before 1.6.16 and 1.7.x before 1.7.1 has Pre-authentication SQL injection in the virtuser_query plugin via a preg_replace() backslash escape bypass.
items.ai_summary: CVE-2026-48842 is a pre-authentication SQL injection vulnerability in Roundcube Webmail 1.6.x before 1.6.16 and 1.7.x before 1.7.1. An attacker can exploit this vulnerability by sending a specially crafted request to the virtuser_query plugin, bypassing the preg_replace() backslash escape mechanism. This allows the attacker to inject malicious SQL code, potentially leading to unauthorized access to user data or even complete control of the webmail system. To remediate this vulnerability, Roundcube Webmail administrators should update to version 1.6.16 or later for 1.6.x, or version 1.7.1 or later for 1.7.x. Additionally, it is recommended to disable the virtuser_query plugin until a patch is applied. Users should also be cautious when clicking on suspicious links or opening attachments from unknown sources, as this vulnerability can be exploited through phishing attacks.

items.description: A vulnerability was found in SourceCodester Student Grades Management System 1.0. Affected is an unknown function of the file grades.php. Performing a manipulation of the argument student_id results in improper authorization. The attack may be initiated remotely. The exploit has been made public and could be used.

items.title: CVE-2026-9482: A vulnerability has been found in Edimax EW-7438RPn 1.31. This impacts the funct...
items.description: A vulnerability has been found in Edimax EW-7438RPn 1.31. This impacts the function formSDHCP of the file /goform/formSDHCP. Such manipulation of the argument submit-url leads to stack-based buffer overflow. The attack can be launched remotely. The exploit has been disclosed to the public and may be used. The vendor was contacted early about this disclosure but did not respond in any way.

items.title: CVE-2026-9481: A flaw has been found in Edimax EW-7438RPn 1.31. This affects the function formS...
items.description: A flaw has been found in Edimax EW-7438RPn 1.31. This affects the function formStats of the file /goform/formStats. This manipulation of the argument submit-url causes stack-based buffer overflow. The attack can be initiated remotely. The exploit has been published and may be used. The vendor was contacted early about this disclosure but did not respond in any way.

items.title: CVE-2026-9480: A vulnerability was detected in Edimax EW-7438RPn 1.31. The impacted element is ...
items.description: A vulnerability was detected in Edimax EW-7438RPn 1.31. The impacted element is the function formrefresh of the file /goform/formrefresh. The manipulation of the argument submit-url results in stack-based buffer overflow. It is possible to launch the attack remotely. The exploit is now public and may be used. The vendor was contacted early about this disclosure but did not respond in any way.

items.title: DentaQuest data breach  exposed info of 2.6 million accounts
items.description: A data breach at the dental benefits administrator DentaQuest has reportedly exposed the sensitive data of 2.6 million accounts. [...]

items.title: Russia seeks to label two anti-Kremlin hacker groups as ‘extremist’
items.description: The groups have previously claimed responsibility for cyberattacks targeting critical infrastructure and government institutions in Russia and Belarus.

items.title: Supreme Court rules FCC fines punishing telecom giants for sharing location data were legal
items.description: The Trump administration had backed the FCC’s position and, apart from Justice Clarence Thomas, the high court agreed.

items.title: Cisco Patches CVE-2026-20230 in Unified CM as Exploit Code Goes Public
items.description: Cisco has patched a bug in Unified Communications Manager that lets an unauthenticated attacker on the network write files to the box and, from there, climb to root. It is tracked as CVE-2026-20230, and proof-of-concept exploit code is already public. Cisco's PSIRT says it has not seen the flaw used in attacks yet. The PoC shortens that runway. The flaw is a server-side request forgery.

items.title: UN food agency discloses breach affecting 600,000 Gaza households
items.description: The United Nations' World Food Programme (WFP), the world's largest humanitarian organization, revealed over the weekend that its self-registration application (SRA) for Palestine was breached. [...]

items.title: New IronWorm malware hits 36 packages in npm supply-chain attack
items.description: A new supply-chain attack has infected 36 packages on the Node Package Manager (npm) index with infostealer malware called IronWorm. [...]

items.title: Claude Code GitHub Action Flaw Let One Malicious Issue Hijack Repositories
items.description: A security researcher found a flaw in Anthropic's Claude Code GitHub Action that let an attacker take over vulnerable public repositories running it, with nothing more than a single opened GitHub issue. Because Anthropic's own action repo used the same workflow, a working attack could have pushed malicious code into the action itself and onto the projects downstream that pull it. RyotaK of GMO

items.title: Agentic AI Is Transforming Defense, But Only Secure IT Infrastructure Will Maximize It
items.description: Over the past several weeks, the cybersecurity community has been reminded how quickly frontier and agentic AI in defense networks can challenge our assumptions. When Anthropic's Claude Mythos model was made available to a limited set of organizations as a technical preview, it was reported that an unauthorized group claimed that it had gained access within hours. The incident, if true, was

items.title: Offroad Emerges From Stealth With $7 Million to Tackle Enterprise Identity Risk
items.description: As AI agents, machine identities, and third-party applications multiply across enterprises, Offroad is betting autonomous security agents can restore control over an increasingly unmanageable identity landscape. The post Offroad Emerges From Stealth With $7 Million to Tackle Enterprise Identity Risk appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: CISA directive for AI executive order to be released this week, Andersen says
items.description: The binding operational directive will focus in part on “vulnerability alleviation and vulnerability management,” Andersen said in remarks delivered at the TechNet Cyber conference in Baltimore.

items.title: Webinar Today: Third-Party Risk in Practice – Where Programs Break Down and How to Respond
items.description: Join this live webinar as we examine the gap between how organizations think their third-party risk programs are performing and what’s actually happening in practice. The post Webinar Today: Third-Party Risk in Practice – Where Programs Break Down and How to Respond appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Willow Raises $7 Million for Securing Autonomous AI Agents
items.description: Willow (formerly Webrix) emerged from stealth mode with an access platform designed to secure enterprise AI agents. The post Willow Raises $7 Million for Securing Autonomous AI Agents appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Bugcrowd Launches EU Data Residency Option For Evolving Data Sovereignty Needs
items.description: Organizations are growing serious about what nation’s rules apply to their data. Experts point to geopolitical tensions as a main contributing factor.

items.title: Hackers Are After the Gaps in Your Vulnerability Program: Here's Their Playbook
items.description: Threat actors are actively teaching newcomers how to find, exploit, and profit from vulnerable systems. Flare explores what a popular underground hacking tutorial reveals about modern attacker workflows. [...]

items.title: ThreatsDay Bulletin: AI Agents Gone Wrong, Sketchy C2 Tools, ClickFix Tricks, JS Backdoors & 20+ New Stories
items.description: It got stupid again. The internet still feels held together with tape. Bad plugins, old bugs, fake tools, trusted apps doing shady things. Same mess, new wrapper. And now the weird stuff is normal. Forums go down and come back worse. Cheap hackers get better toys. AI starts breaking real systems. Great. Read the whole thing before it ruins your week anyway. Unauthenticated

items.title: UN food agency investigates breach exposing data of Gaza aid recipients
items.description: In a message sent to aid recipients via Telegram over the weekend, the World Food Programme (WFP) said that "unauthorized parties" had accessed data stored in its self-registration application in Gaza.

items.title: Microsoft blames unexpected Windows driver updates on caching issue
items.description: On Wednesday, Microsoft fixed an issue that caused some Windows devices to install driver updates without notice despite policies configured to prevent auto-updates. [...]

items.title: Researcher publishes GitHub token-stealing exploit, blames Microsoft’s disclosure process
items.description: The security researcher, Ammar Askar, released the new proof-of-concept exploit on his personal blog — alongside the public tracker for issues in VS Code — giving a GitHub security contact roughly one hour's notice beforehand.

items.title: Gemini Voice Assistant Hijacked via Messaging Notifications
items.description: Attackers could have triggered dangerous actions, including controlling smart home devices via Google Home and starting Zoom video calls. The post Gemini Voice Assistant Hijacked via Messaging Notifications appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Police dismantles fake ID marketplace used by migrant smugglers
items.description: French and Spanish authorities took down an online marketplace selling fake identity documents to migrant smuggling rings operating within the European Union. [...]

items.title: China-Linked TA4922 Expands Phishing Attacks to U.K., Germany, Italy, and South Africa
items.description: A new China-linked cybercrime group known as TA4922 has expanded its targeting focus to target European organizations in the U.K., Germany, Italy, and South Africa. These efforts have been complemented by a "rapid operational tempo" and a continually evolving malware arsenal comprising known families like ValleyRAT (aka Winos 4.0) and Atlas RAT (aka AtlasCross RAT), as well as previously

items.title: Mirasvit Vulnerability Exploited to Execute Code on Magento Servers
items.description: A flaw in the Full Page Cache Warmer extension can be exploited without authentication via serialized PHP object payloads. The post Mirasvit Vulnerability Exploited to Execute Code on Magento Servers appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Chinese Cybercrime Group in Spotlight for Record Campaign Pace
items.description: Relying on social engineering, the hacking group engages in credential phishing, malware distribution, and fraud activities. The post Chinese Cybercrime Group in Spotlight for Record Campaign Pace appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: FlutterShell Backdoor Spreads to macOS via Malicious Google and YouTube Ads
items.description: Cybersecurity researchers have shed light on a macOS malvertising campaign codenamed Operation FlutterBridge that spreads a new backdoor called FlutterShell. According to Palo Alto Networks Unit 42, the campaign is said to be the next stage of a previously reported activity cluster dubbed JSCoreRunner (aka FileRipple) in late August 2025. The cybercrime group behind the two attack chains is

items.title: Cisco warns of critical Unified CM flaw with PoC exploit code
items.description: Cisco has released security updates to patch a critical-severity Unified Communications Manager (Unified CM) flaw that allows attackers to gain root privileges. [...]

items.title: Over 1.4 Million Accounts Disrupted in Cybercrime Crackdown
items.description: Law enforcement and tech companies disrupted infrastructure linked to scammers operating across Southeast Asia. The post Over 1.4 Million Accounts Disrupted in Cybercrime Crackdown appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Fake Sites Mimicking Open-Source Tools Rank High on Google to Deliver Malware via TDS
items.description: Cybersecurity researchers have flagged a large-scale operation that impersonates open-source and freeware projects to funnel unsuspecting users through a Traffic Distribution System (TDS) and deliver malware families like Remus Stealer, AnimateClipper, and the SessionGate framework. "The sites are well-designed and often look like legitimate project portals at a glance, sometimes referencing

items.title: Hackers Spied on a Stock Exchange Executive's Outlook Mailbox for Five Months
items.description: Unknown attackers spent at least five months inside the Outlook mailbox of a senior executive at a major global stock exchange, copying the inbox out in small, repeated batches and routing it through Dropbox and OneDrive so the traffic blended into normal cloud activity. Symantec and Carbon Black's Threat Hunter Team reported the campaign this week. This points to espionage, not a money grab:

items.title: Cisco Warns of Available PoC for Critical Unified CM Vulnerability
items.description: The high-severity flaw can be exploited remotely, without authentication, in server-side request forgery (SSRF) attacks. The post Cisco Warns of Available PoC for Critical Unified CM Vulnerability appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: VS Code Vulnerability Allows One-Click GitHub Token Theft
items.description: A researcher has disclosed the full details of the vulnerability and released a PoC without notifying Microsoft in advance. The post VS Code Vulnerability Allows One-Click GitHub Token Theft appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.description: I&&#x23;x26;&#x23;39;ve been using the GnuWin32 CoreUtils for Windows for many years now (it gives you many *nix core commands on Windows).

items.description: The U.S. Department of Justice (DoJ) on Wednesday announced the results of a sweeping action undertaken by government authorities and private sector companies to combat cyber-enabled and cryptocurrency fraud targeting Americans. The "Disruption Week" operation began May 18, 2026, leading to the takedown of millions of social media, email, and internet access accounts used by transnational

items.title: Pakistan Spies on Afghan Finance Ministry With Xeno RAT
items.description: Despite broadly connected digital infrastructure, standard fare TTPs are enough to cause trouble for Afghanistan's porous cybersecurity.

items.description: A Chinese-speaking cybercrime group has expanded its targeting to the European space, deploying previously undocumented malware and the Atlas backdoor. [...]

items.title: Attackers Use AI to Automate EDR Evasion Testing
items.description: Python scripts were used to test malware against endpoint detection and response agents from Sophos, CrowdStrike, and Windows Defender.

items.description: The U.S. Treasury's Office of Foreign Assets Control (OFAC) has announced sanctions against Nobitex, Iran's largest cryptocurrency exchange, for facilitating payments related to terrorist activities. [...]

items.title: CISA warns of cyberattacks targeting fuel tank monitoring systems
items.description: CISA, the FBI, the NSA, the Department of Energy, and other US government partners are warning that hackers are targeting internet-exposed automatic tank gauge (ATG) systems used to monitor fuel and liquid storage tanks across various critical infrastructure sectors. [...]

items.title: Tropical Blend: Cyber & Politics Ramp Up Across Latin America
items.description: China-linked espionage groups have attacked at least a dozen nations in the region, gathering information on maritime shipping, oil production, and other geopolitical interests.

items.title: WhatsApp, Slack Notifications Could Hijack Google Gemini on Android
items.description: A single poisoned notification from WhatsApp, Slack, SMS, Signal, Instagram, or Messenger could have hijacked Google Gemini's voice assistant on Android and made it open a victim's connected windows, fake a message from their boss, push the phone into a Zoom call, or quietly poison its long-term memory. No malicious app on the phone is required. The assistant just had to treat a hostile

items.title: Cyber Insurance Rates Are Dropping, but Exclusions Widen
items.description: Cyber insurance coverage is slowly changing, and some policies may not provide coverage for social engineering attacks like ClickFix.

items.title: Coding Gaffe Exposes Microsoft 365 Accounts to Widespread Takeover
items.description: A disabled security setting meant to protect authentication across Android versions of key apps like Word, PowerPoint, and Excel paved the way for attackers to steal logins and data.

items.title: Coralogix Raises $200M at $1.6B Valuation to Scale AI Observability Platform
items.description: Coralogix offers a full-stack observability platform that unifies logs, metrics, traces, security, and AI observability. The post Coralogix Raises $200M at $1.6B Valuation to Scale AI Observability Platform appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Continuing Scans for swagger.json, (Wed, Jun 3rd)
items.description: Enterprise applications often still use complex standards like SOAP for web services. The big advantage of SOAP is its tight and extensive standards, which enable interoperability across an enterprise governed by web services. The disadvantage of SOAP: First, while it is de facto usually used over HTTP, it does not leverage HTTP, leading to unnecessary complexity. Secondly, kids don&&#x23;x26;&#x23;39;t RTFM, and developers these days tend not to appreciate the art of careful system design; they rather throw code at an IDE to see what sticks, if they don&&#x23;x26;&#x23;39;t vibe code it anyway.

items.title: Malicious Notifications Could Trick Google Gemini Users
items.description: A prompt injection flaw in Google Gemini's voice assistant let attackers hide malicious commands in notifications, enabling social engineering and more.

items.title: Global Stock Exchange Hit by Monthslong Email Campaign
items.description: A threat actor got a near-continuous view into an influential finance executive's email inbox, thanks to clever use of legitimate, native Windows tools.

items.description: Once targeting just Microsoft 365, the phishing-as-a-service platform now aims at AWS, Okta, and Russian platforms, while relying on device code phishing.

New Wave Of Phishing Emails with SVG Files, (Tue, Jun 2nd)
items.description: For a few days, my SANS ISC mailbox is flooded with emails that delivers SVG files. An SVG ("Scalable Vector Graphic") is a web-friendly vector file format used for graphics and icons. No URL in the body, just â€œan imageâ€, that&#x27s the perfect way to deliver some malicious content. This isn&#x27t the first time that we see this technique used by threat actors[1].

items.title: Watering Hole Attacks Push ScanBox Keylogger
items.description: Researchers uncover a watering hole attack likely carried out by APT TA423, which attempts to plant the ScanBox JavaScript-based reconnaissance tool.

items.title: Tentacles of ‘0ktapus’ Threat Group Victimize 130 Firms
items.description: Over 130 companies tangled in sprawling phishing campaign that spoofed a multi-factor authentication system.

items.description: Lockbit is by far this summer’s most prolific ransomware group, trailed by two offshoots of the Conti group.

items.title: Cybercriminals Are Selling Access to Chinese Surveillance Cameras
items.description: Tens of thousands of cameras have failed to patch a critical, 11-month-old CVE, leaving thousands of organizations exposed.

items.title: Twitter Whistleblower Complaint: The TL;DR Version
items.description: Twitter is blasted for security and privacy lapses by the company’s former head of security who alleges the social media giant’s actions amount to a national security risk.

items.title: Firewall Bug Under Active Attack Triggers CISA Warning
items.description: CISA is warning that Palo Alto Networks’ PAN-OS is under active attack and needs to be patched ASAP.

items.title: Fake Reservation Links Prey on Weary Travelers
items.description: Fake travel reservations are exacting more pain from the travel weary, already dealing with the misery of canceled flights and overbooked hotels.

items.title: iPhone Users Urged to Update to Patch 2 Zero-Days
items.description: Separate fixes to macOS and iOS patch respective flaws in the kernel and WebKit that can allow threat actors to take over devices and are under attack.

items.title: Google Patches Chrome’s Fifth Zero-Day of the Year
items.description: An insufficient validation input flaw, one of 11 patched in an update this week, could allow for arbitrary code execution and is under active attack.
